title: Search results stay stale after filter reset
bug_description: After applying filters and then resetting them, the UI still shows stale filtered results instead of returning to the full result set.
expected_behavior: Resetting filters should clear filter state, trigger a fresh query or state recompute, and show the full result set.
actual_behavior: The filter controls reset visually, but the results list remains narrowed until a manual refresh or a second interaction.

preconditions: A local UI environment is running with seeded searchable data
At least one filter reduces the visible result set
steps: Open the search page and apply a filter that reduces the list
Use the reset or clear-all control
Observe whether the result set returns to the unfiltered state immediately
reproduction.expected_behavior: The full unfiltered result set appears right after the reset action.
reproduction.actual_behavior: The UI state clears, but the old filtered results remain visible.

logs: UI event shows reset action fired, but result state did not recompute or refetch

manual_checks: Re-run the filter-reset flow and confirm the list returns to the full dataset
Confirm a second filter application still works after the reset path

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if reset starts causing empty or duplicated result sets
rollback.notes: Keep the last stable search interaction behavior documented with screenshots or a recorded repro.

scope_notes: Keep the fix inside search state wiring and the filter reset path. Do not rework unrelated pagination, sorting, or backend search logic unless the root cause proves it is necessary.

notes.root_cause_hint: Likely stale derived state, missing dependency tracking, or reset action not invalidating cached results
notes.risk_hint: A broad reset fix could break pagination, default sorting, or optimistic UI behavior
similar_bugs: sort change not refreshing cached results
reset clears controls but not query params
